sections.paragraph:
Kal and Piel to gather, to collect, prop. things lying on the ground, e.g. cars of corn, Rth 2:3, , 15 stones, Gen 31:46 flowers, Son 6:2 manna, Exo 16:14, seq. Once used of collecting money, Gen 47:14. [“Arab. لقط to collect any thing lying on the ground; Syr. ܠܩܰܛ Peal and Pael, used of collecting wood; Sam. , but Nasor. ܠܓܛ to collect.” Thes.]

Pual, Isa 27:12, and-

Hithpael, Jud. 11:3 , to be gathered, assembled (of persons), Arab. and Aram. id.

Hence יַלְקוּט and-
